title: MANGA Has anyone seen this version
display_title: MANGA Has anyone seen this version
wikitext:
[]: Has anyone seen this version?             Quote Post by machinamar » Sat Apr 21, 2007 10:28 am There is yet another version of Iron Maiden 2nd floating around ... I am interested in this one because it looks very much like the work of Mine Yoshizaki (Sgt. Frog) but I can't find any information on it other than scrambled translation of the Eva store. [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=839&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=840&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=984&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] (As always with work like Mine Yoshizaki's, tolerance of fan service is required.) So, yeah, has anyone even heard of this version?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Apr 21, 2007 11:57 am Looks prettier than that other wun.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 21, 2007 2:18 pm It's not a series, just volumes of omake comics. There's nothing narratively interesting about them. Just zany hijinks featuring your favorite characters by a variety of artists. There's omake for the main story, GOS, GOS2, NGE2, ISIK, and more floating around on store shelves.

Article: MANGA Has anyone seen this version
[]: Has anyone seen this version?             Quote Post by machinamar » Sat Apr 21, 2007 10:28 am There is yet another version of Iron Maiden 2nd floating around ... I am interested in this one because it looks very much like the work of Mine Yoshizaki (Sgt. Frog) but I can't find any information on it other than scrambled translation of the Eva store. [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=839&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=840&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] [url]http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=984&genre=30_book&page=2&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0[/url] (As always with work like Mine Yoshizaki's, tolerance of fan service is required.) So, yeah, has anyone even heard of this version?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Apr 21, 2007 11:57 am Looks prettier than that other wun.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 21, 2007 2:18 pm It's not a series, just volumes of omake comics. There's nothing narratively interesting about them. Just zany hijinks featuring your favorite characters by a variety of artists. There's omake for the main story, GOS, GOS2, NGE2, ISIK, and more floating around on store shelves.
